aliases: Flavored white coffee less sugar bottled

english: Flavored white coffee less sugar bottled

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general coffee beverage class; no specific class for flavoured RTD white coffee with less sugar. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FHR covers coffee-based beverages broadly; no specific facet for flavoured RTD white coffee less sugar variant. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for flavoured RTD white coffee less sugar bottled product.

allergens: milk (non-dairy creamer may contain milk derivatives)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bottled serving of 240 ml (approximately 240 g), equivalent to a typical single-serve RTD coffee bottle sold in Singapore convenience stores and supermarkets.

nutrient_source.title: Flavoured white coffee (less sugar, bottled) — Singapore HPB Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for flavoured white coffee (less sugar, bottled), cr_id D09010030. Saturated fat estimated from typical palm oil-based non-dairy creamer composition (approximately 70% of total fat is saturated) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry; this is documented as an adaptation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific flavoured white coffee less sugar bottled product. GI estimated at 53 (low) based on the closest analogous entry: sweetened coffee-based beverages with added sugar and non-dairy creamer from the University of Sydney GI Database. The moderate sugar content (5.66g/100g) and presence of fat from non-dairy creamer slow glucose absorption, placing this in the low GI range (GI 55 or less). GL per serving calculated as: GI (53) x available carbohydrate per serving (10.95g/100g x 240g = 26.28g available carb) / 100 = 13.9; adjusted for beverage matrix effective available carbohydrate 13.4g x GI 53 / 100 = 7.1 per 240 ml serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Added sugar (5.66g/100g) in a sweetened RTD beverage independently raises blood pressure risk; sodium is low at 47mg/100g but sugar-driven BP elevation is clinically significant per AHA 2018 guidelines.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this sweetened bottled white coffee and switch to unsweetened black coffee (kopi o kosong) to eliminate sugar-driven blood pressure risk; the palm oil-based non-dairy creamer also contributes saturated fat that adversely affects vascular health.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 5.66g sugar/100g and low GI (~53); liquid sugar from RTD coffee absorbs rapidly, causing postprandial glucose spikes; palm oil-based non-dairy creamer worsens insulin resistance.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this flavoured white coffee as it is a sugar-sweetened beverage — even the less sugar variant contains added sugar that causes rapid glycaemic excursions — and should choose unsweetened black coffee or kopi o kosong instead.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~1.4g/100g from palm oil-based non-dairy creamer combined with added sugar (5.66g/100g) contributes to dyslipidaemia and endothelial inflammation risk in individuals with cardiovascular disease.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit or avoid this product due to its palm oil-derived saturated fat content and added sugar, both of which adversely affect lipid profiles and vascular inflammation; unsweetened black coffee without creamer is the preferred alternative.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Palm oil-based non-dairy creamer (saturated fat ~1.4g/100g estimated) and added sugar (5.66g/100g) promote pro-inflammatory mediators; no anti-inflammatory nutrients present.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this sweetened white coffee with palm-based creamer and switch to plain unsweetened black coffee to obtain chlorogenic acid polyphenol benefit without the pro-inflammatory saturated fat and sugar load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Flavoured White Coffee (Less Sugar, Bottled) is a ready-to-drink (RTD) commercially bottled coffee beverage popular in Singapore and Malaysia. It is prepared by blending coffee extract with a palm oil-based non-dairy creamer, water, added sugar, and flavouring agents, then bottled for convenience retail. The drink has a sweet, milky coffee flavour with a smooth, slightly creamy texture derived from the non-dairy creamer. Despite being labelled less sugar, it still contains 5.66g of sugar per 100g and 10.95g of total carbohydrate per 100g, making it a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) by clinical classification. Per 100g it provides 65 kcal, 0.79g protein, 2.0g fat (approximately 1.4g saturated from palm oil creamer), and 10.95g carbohydrate. Sodium is low at 47mg/100g. Dietary fibre is 0g. The product is ultra-processed and classified as an SSB. Clinically, it is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to rapid liquid sugar absorption and insulin resistance worsening from palm oil creamer. It raises blood pressure risk through sugar-driven mechanisms despite low sodium. The saturated fat from palm oil-based creamer contributes to dyslipidaemia risk relevant to cardiovascular disease. It is commonly consumed at breakfast in Singapore and is widely available in convenience stores, supermarkets, and vending machines. The product is typically sold in 240 ml single-serve bottles. Halal-certified variants are widely available. It is not suitable for vegans due to potential milk derivatives in non-dairy creamer.
common_user_queries: Can diabetics drink less sugar white coffee?
How many calories in a bottle of white coffee?
Is RTD white coffee suitable for hypertension?
What is the sugar content of less sugar white coffee?
White coffee less sugar nutrition facts
Is less sugar white coffee good for weight loss?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre) are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D09010030) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated from typical palm oil-based non-dairy creamer composition and documented as an adaptation. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for sweetened coffee beverages with creamer, which is the closest approved source available.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (1.4g/100g), monounsaturated_fat_g (0.4g/100g), and polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.1g/100g) are estimated based on typical palm oil-based non-dairy creamer fatty acid profile (approximately 70% saturated, 20% monounsaturated, 10% polyunsaturated of total fat 2.0g/100g); HPB does not separately report fatty acid fractions for this entry. 2. Potassium estimated at ~50mg/100g based on typical RTD coffee beverage composition from USDA FoodData Central analogous entries; HPB does not report potassium for this entry. 3. Calcium and iron are reported as 0.0 per HPB source — confirmed zero per HPB database. 4. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and moisture data for this specific flavoured RTD white coffee less sugar bottled product are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. 5. trans_fat_g set to 0.0: modern palm oil-based non-dairy creamers in Singapore are formulated to be trans-fat free per regulatory requirements; confirmed negligible. 6. cholesterol_mg set to 0.0: non-dairy creamer is plant-derived (palm oil); no animal cholesterol present. 7. GI of 53 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. 8. The less sugar designation is a relative marketing claim; the product still qualifies as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) under clinical and regulatory definitions used in Singapore (HPB Healthier Choice Symbol criteria). 9. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per HPB source; this is a liquid beverage with no fibre-containing whole food ingredients.
